skill.name: maxhub-toutiao
skill.description: Query Toutiao articles, videos, users, and comments through MaxHub APIs.
skill.summary: Analyze Toutiao content and user data with authenticated MaxHub API calls.
skill.author: XieWxx

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer correctly identified network access, environment credential use, and shell command examples, but the evidence is documentation for a MaxHub API skill rather than hidden executable code. Weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, entropy, and most backtick execution alerts are false positives from Markdown syntax and Chinese text. Publish with a warning because the skill sends user queries and a bearer token to a third-party API and instructs agents to run curl commands.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Third-Party API Access
medium_findings.description: The skill requires MAXHUB_API_KEY and instructs use of an Authorization bearer header against https://www.aconfig.cn. This is expected for the skill, but it sends the user's API key and query details to a third-party service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable, bearer header, and remote base URL are explicit. The behavior matches the advertised MaxHub API purpose, so the risk is disclosure and dependency risk rather than confirmed malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Shell-Based API Calls Require Careful Parameter Handling
medium_findings.description: The skill shows curl commands with endpoint and parameter placeholders and tells the agent to execute planned queries autonomously. If an implementation interpolates user-provided values into shell commands without quoting, command injection could occur.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains command templates and autonomous execution instructions, but no executable source code was found. The concern depends on how the host agent constructs curl commands.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Ruby Execution
low_findings.description: Most external command alerts come from Markdown code fences or inline code that document installation, API paths, and curl examples. These files are not Ruby source and do not execute by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged backticks appear in Markdown fences, inline code spans, tables, and endpoint documentation. No Ruby files or Ruby backtick operators were present in the scanned file list.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The lines flagged as weak cryptography are Markdown table rows, headings, and API response descriptions. I did not find evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines shows documentation text and parameter tables only. There is no hashing or encryption logic in those locations.

low_findings.title: Entropy and Reconnaissance Alerts Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: README_CN.md contains Chinese documentation, which explains the entropy alert. System reconnaissance alerts point to example IDs, offsets, and URL parameters rather than host inspection commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is human-readable API documentation. No evidence found of uname, whoami, hostname, filesystem probing, or similar reconnaissance behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bearer Token Sent with Curl to External API
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented curl pattern sends Authorization: Bearer credentials to the MaxHub API. This is a normal integration pattern, but users should understand the credential and data-sharing implications.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The bearer header and external URL are shown together in the same API access block. The pattern is intentional and user-visible, not hidden.

content.value_statement: Teams need fast access to Toutiao article, video, user, and comment data. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to query MaxHub APIs and summarize the results.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether MAXHUB_API_KEY is configured before API use.
Routes article, video, user, and comment requests to documented Toutiao endpoints.
Supports get_user_id, get_user_info, get_article_info, get_video_info, and get_comments workflows.
Formats responses in Chinese or English based on the user language.
Explains common API errors, empty results, and retry behavior.
Combines multiple endpoint calls for comparison or deeper analysis.
limitations: Requires a MaxHub API key from www.aconfig.cn.
Depends on third-party API availability, permissions, and rate limits.
Covers only the Toutiao endpoints documented in the skill files.
Returned data is third-party reference data and may be incomplete or delayed.

use_cases.title: Monitor News Content
use_cases.description: Track article and video metadata to understand current Toutiao topics and engagement signals.

use_cases.title: Review Creator Profiles
use_cases.description: Look up user IDs and profile information before comparing creator activity or audience context.

use_cases.title: Analyze Comment Threads
use_cases.description: Fetch comments for selected posts and summarize audience reactions, themes, and follow-up questions.

prompt_templates.title: Check Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Check whether my MaxHub API key is configured for Toutiao queries. If it is missing, show the setup steps.

prompt_templates.title: Fetch One Article
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the Toutiao article information for this article link and summarize the title, author, publish time, and engagement data.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Two Videos
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these two Toutiao video links. Show the key metadata, engagement differences, and likely audience interest.

prompt_templates.title: Build Content Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these Toutiao posts and related creators. Use the available endpoints, compare performance, and provide a concise monitoring report.

output_examples.input: Get details for a Toutiao article link.
output_examples.output: A concise article summary with title, author, publish time, engagement fields, source link, and any missing data notes.

output: Short interpretation of likely audience response

output_examples.output: A theme summary that groups common reactions, highlights sentiment signals, and notes that results depend on returned API data.

best_practices: Use specific article, video, or profile URLs when available.
Keep API keys in secure environment configuration and never paste them into prompts.
Ask for comparisons or reports only after identifying the content items to inspect.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill without permission to send data to MaxHub APIs.
Do not treat returned third-party data as verified ground truth.
Do not request unsupported endpoints or unrelated social platforms.

faq.question: What API key does this skill need?
faq.answer: It needs MAXHUB_API_KEY from MaxHub at www.aconfig.cn.

faq.question: Can it work without network access?
faq.answer: No. The skill depends on MaxHub network API calls for live Toutiao data.

faq.question: Does it support Chinese and English?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill instructs responses to match the user language.

faq.question: What Toutiao data can it query?
faq.answer: It documents article details, video details, user lookup, user details, and comments.

faq.question: Is the data official Toutiao data?
faq.answer: No. The skill states that data comes through third-party APIs and is for reference.

faq.question: What should users avoid sharing?
faq.answer: Users should not paste API keys or private data into prompts or output.
